message:
perf(extensions-js): add call_global_void() and refactor pijs_workload
to eliminate per-call JS parsing overhead

extensions_js.rs — new call_global_void() method:
- Add `pub async fn call_global_void(&self, name: &str) -> Result<()>`
  to PiJsRuntime that invokes a named global JS function with no
  arguments and no return value
- Uses rquickjs globals().get(name) → Function::call::<(), ()>(())
  instead of eval(), avoiding the QuickJS parser/compile phase on
  every invocation — significant for hot-path extension callbacks
- Respects interrupt_budget.reset() and drain_jobs() for async safety
- Properly maps rquickjs errors through the existing anyhow chain

pijs_workload.rs — benchmark refactoring:
- Replace eval(BENCH_TOOL_CALL) loop with call_global_void(BENCH_BEGIN_FN)
  to exercise the new fast-path in benchmarks
- Consolidate BENCH_TOOL_SETUP + BENCH_TOOL_CALL into single
  BENCH_TOOL_SETUP constant that registers the named global function
  once during setup, then the benchmark loop calls it by name
- More accurately reflects real extension dispatch patterns where
  functions are registered once and called repeatedly

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
